provenance.author: James / Giblets Forge Ltd
provenance.context: Core gameplay knowledge capsule for CobbleWright - captures full Minecraft gameplay loop so an AI companion bot can reason about aims, survival, building, mob threat, and time cycles.

intent.primary: Give CobbleWright (or any consuming agent) a self-contained, structured model of what Minecraft is, how it's played, and how to survive and progress in it - usable as grounding knowledge without needing external lookups.
intent.problem_solved: CobbleWright needs a single reference capsule to reason about player state, threat level, time of day, and build/survive priorities instead of scattered or partial game knowledge.

game_summary.genre: Open-world sandbox survival / creative building game
game_summary.core_aim: There is no single mandated win condition. The implicit long-arc goal is: survive, gather resources, build shelter and infrastructure, progress through tool/armor tiers, explore dimensions, and optionally defeat the Ender Dragon (The End) and Wither as the game's two major boss end-game milestones. Most play is open-ended - survival, building, and exploration are goals in themselves.

modes.survival: Health, hunger, resource-gathering, and mob threat are all active. This is the real gameplay loop.
modes.creative: Unlimited resources, flight, no damage/hunger. Pure building/design mode, not survival.
modes.hardcore: Survival rules, but death is permanent (world locks).
modes.adventure: Restricted block interaction, used for custom maps.

breakdown.day: ~10 minutes - safer period, hostile mobs mostly don't spawn in lit areas, best time to gather resources, farm, explore
breakdown.dusk: Sky reddens, a warning phase - get to shelter, light up your base
breakdown.night: ~7 minutes - hostile mobs spawn freely in darkness; most dangerous period unless area is well-lit and enclosed
breakdown.dawn: Sky lightens, hostile mobs (except some, e.g. drowned, phantoms) start burning in sunlight or despawning

day_night_cycle.practical_rule: Light level matters more than clock time - hostile mobs spawn on any block with light level 0 (in older/vanilla logic, <=7 in some versions) regardless of day or night, so a dark cave at noon is just as dangerous as an unlit surface at midnight. Torches, lanterns, and glowstone are the core defense against spawns, not walls alone.

core_stats.health: 10 hearts (20 HP) by default. Regenerates slowly if hunger is high enough; damaged by mobs, falls, fire, drowning, lava, starvation.
core_stats.hunger: 20 points (10 drumstick icons). Depletes via movement/actions. At 0, starts draining health on higher difficulties. Must eat food to refill.
core_stats.armor: Reduces incoming damage. Tiered: leather < gold < chainmail < iron < diamond < netherite.

first_hour_checklist: Punch trees for wood - first and most urgent resource
Craft a crafting table, wooden tools (pickaxe first - unlocks stone tier)
Mine stone with wooden pickaxe, upgrade tools to stone
Find/kill food source early (animals, or gather food from grass/berries)
Build or find basic shelter before first nightfall - even a dirt hole with a placed block over the entrance works
Get a furnace + basic light source (torches need coal or charcoal + stick)
Progress toward iron: iron ore needs a stone pickaxe to mine, then smelt in furnace
survival_priorities.key_early_goal: Reach iron tier ASAP - iron unlocks armor, better weapons, and (critically) a shield and access to enchanting-adjacent progression.

building.why_build: Shelter is the #1 survival requirement (blocks mob spawns/attacks), but building is also a core creative pillar of the game independent of survival need.
basic_shelter_rules: Fully enclose the space - no gaps >=1 block for mobs to walk/spawn in
Light the interior to spawn-proof it (torches every ~7 blocks or so)
A door or trapdoor for entry keeps most mobs out (zombies can break wooden doors on higher difficulties in some versions - solid block entrances are safer)
Dig into a hillside or go underground for the fastest safe first shelter
progression_builds: Fenced farm + animal pen for renewable food
Proper base with storage (chests), furnace bank, bed
Mine entrance/staircase down to resource-rich layers
Nether portal (obsidian frame + flint & steel) for Nether access
End portal (found in strongholds, needs Eyes of Ender) for The End
building.materials_note: Almost any solid block can be used to build; stone/cobblestone is the reliable early structural material since it's abundant and not flammable (unlike wood, which creepers/lava can ignite).

resource_gathering.core_loop: Chop wood -> mine stone -> mine coal/iron -> smelt -> mine deeper for diamonds/redstone/gold -> (optional) Nether for blaze rods/netherite -> (optional) End for elytra/dragon egg.

key_ores.iron: Tools/armor tier 3, needs stone pickaxe, needs smelting. Commonly found underground in caves and mountainsides.
key_ores.gold: Weakest tool tier but useful for specific items (powered rails, some enchant/trade uses), needs iron pickaxe
key_ores.diamond: Tool tier 4, needs iron pickaxe, found deep
key_ores.netherite: Best tier, found only in the Nether, upgrades diamond gear via smithing table

resource_gathering.food_sources: Farming (wheat/carrots/potatoes/etc.), animal husbandry (cows, pigs, chickens, sheep), fishing, foraging (berries, apples).

hostile_mobs_baddies.zombie: Common night spawn, melee attacker, burns in sunlight (unless in shade/water), moderate threat alone, dangerous in groups/hordes.
hostile_mobs_baddies.skeleton: Ranged bow attacker, burns in sunlight, dangerous at range especially near ledges (can knock player off).
hostile_mobs_baddies.creeper: Silent approach, explodes near player - no ranged warning besides a hiss sound, primary threat to builds since it destroys terrain/structures. Does NOT burn in sunlight, so it's a threat day or night.
hostile_mobs_baddies.spider: Can climb walls, neutral in daylight (only attacks if provoked) but hostile at night regardless of provocation.
hostile_mobs_baddies.enderman: Tall, teleporting, neutral unless you look directly at it or attack it - then very dangerous. Picks up/places blocks. Avoid eye contact to stay safe.
hostile_mobs_baddies.witch: Throws harmful potions (poison, weakness) and drinks healing/buff potions on itself. Found in swamp huts.
hostile_mobs_baddies.drowned: Underwater zombie variant, can carry a trident, dangerous in/near water.
hostile_mobs_baddies.phantom: Flying mob that spawns if the player hasn't slept in several in-game days - direct mechanical punishment for avoiding sleep.
hostile_mobs_baddies.slime: Splits into smaller slimes when killed, found in swamps/specific underground conditions, low individual threat.
hostile_mobs_baddies.guardian_elder_guardian: Ocean monument dwellers, ranged laser attack, elder guardians inflict mining fatigue - gatekeepers to ocean monument loot.
hostile_mobs_baddies.blaze: Nether mob, ranged fireballs, drops blaze rods (required for brewing stands and to make Eyes of Ender for The End).
hostile_mobs_baddies.wither_skeleton: Nether fortress mob, melee, causes Wither status effect on hit.
hostile_mobs_baddies.piglin: Nether mob, neutral if player wears gold armor, hostile otherwise, can be bartered with using gold ingots.
hostile_mobs_baddies.boss_wither: Player-summoned boss (soul sand/soul soil + 3 wither skulls), highly dangerous area-damage fight, mid/late-game challenge, drops the Nether Star.
hostile_mobs_baddies.boss_ender_dragon: Found in The End, the game's traditional final boss - defeating it triggers the end-game credits sequence, considered the closest thing Minecraft has to a canonical win condition.

passive_neutral_mobs_goodies.cow: Passive, provides leather and beef, milk from bucket
passive_neutral_mobs_goodies.pig: Passive, provides porkchop, can be ridden with saddle + carrot on a stick
passive_neutral_mobs_goodies.sheep: Passive, provides wool (shearable, renewable) and mutton
passive_neutral_mobs_goodies.chicken: Passive, provides feathers, eggs, chicken meat
passive_neutral_mobs_goodies.horse_donkey: Tameable rideable mounts, horses can be bred/equipped with armor
passive_neutral_mobs_goodies.wolf: Neutral, tameable into a loyal fighting companion dog with bones
passive_neutral_mobs_goodies.cat: Neutral, tameable with fish, repels phantoms and creepers when nearby
passive_neutral_mobs_goodies.villager: Neutral, lives in villages, trades goods/emeralds for items, has professions (farmer, librarian, blacksmith etc.) tied to workstation blocks
passive_neutral_mobs_goodies.iron_golem: Neutral, defends villages against hostile mobs, very strong melee
passive_neutral_mobs_goodies.allay: Passive helper mob, collects matching items and can be used with jukeboxes for note block farms
passive_neutral_mobs_goodies.bee: Neutral, provides honey, pollinates crops, stings if provoked/hive disturbed

dimensions.overworld: Default starting dimension, day/night cycle, most biomes and resources
dimensions.nether: Reached via obsidian portal, hostile fire/lava dimension, source of blaze rods, netherite, and a faster travel shortcut (1 Nether block ~= 8 Overworld blocks)
dimensions.the_end: Reached via End portal (found in strongholds using Eyes of Ender to locate), home of the Ender Dragon boss and, after defeat, End cities with elytra (flight item) loot

progression_summary: 2. Stone tools, basic shelter, survive first night
5. Nether access - blaze rods, netherite upgrade, Wither fight (optional)
6. Eyes of Ender -> locate stronghold -> End portal
7. Defeat Ender Dragon -> access The End cities -> elytra
8. Open-ended post-game: mega-builds, redstone engineering, full survival mastery, or just continued exploration/creativity

trust.note: Static reference knowledge, not time-decaying - general game mechanics don't go stale the way project-state facts do. Re-verify against current game version if used for a specific up-to-date Minecraft release, as mob behaviour/mechanics have changed across major updates.
